children.heading: Establishment of State and Local Implementation Grant Program
children.label: a Establishment of State and Local Implementation Grant Program
children.text: The Assistant Secretary, in consultation with the First Responder Network Authority, shall take such action as is necessary to establish a grant program to make grants to States to assist State, regional, tribal, and local jurisdictions to identify, plan, and implement the most efficient and effective way for such jurisdictions to utilize and integrate the infrastructure, equipment, and other architecture associated with the nationwide public safety broadband network to satisfy the wireless communications and data services needs of that jurisdiction, including with regards to coverage, siting, and other needs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of any activity carried out using a grant under this section may not exceed 80 percent of the eligible costs of carrying out that activity, as determined by the Assistant Secretary, in consultation with the First Responder Network Authority.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 2 Waiver
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may waive, in whole or in part, the requirements of paragraph (1) for good cause shown if the Assistant Secretary determines that such a waiver is in the public interest.
Defining eligible costs for purposes of subsection (b)(1).
Determining the scope of eligible activities for grant funding under this section.
Prioritizing grants for activities that ensure coverage in rural as well as urban areas.

children.heading: Certification and designation of officer or governmental body
children.label: d Certification and designation of officer or governmental body
children.text: In carrying out the grant program established under this section, the Assistant Secretary shall require each State to certify in its application for grant funds that the State has designated a single officer or governmental body to serve as the coordinator of implementation of the grant funds.

children.label: A
children.text: notice of the completion of the request for proposal process;

children.label: B
children.text: details of the proposed plan for buildout of the nationwide, interoperable broadband network in such State; and

children.label: C
children.text: the funding level for the State as determined by the NTIA.

children.label: A
children.text: participate in the deployment of the nationwide, interoperable broadband network as proposed by the First Responder Network Authority; or

children.label: B
children.text: conduct its own deployment of a radio access network in such State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Upon making a decision to opt-out under paragraph (2)(B), the Governor shall notify the First Responder Network Authority, the NTIA, and the Commission of such decision.

children.heading: State request for proposals
children.label: B State request for proposals
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date on which a Governor provides notice under subparagraph (A), the Governor shall develop and complete requests for proposals for the construction, maintenance, and operation of the radio access network within the State.

children.heading: Submission and approval of alternative plan
children.label: C Submission and approval of alternative plan
I

children.label: II
children.text: interoperability with the nationwide public safety broadband network.

children.heading: Commission approval or disapproval
children.label: ii Commission approval or disapproval
children.text: Upon submission of a State plan under clause (i), the Commission shall either approve or disapprove the plan.

children.label: I
children.text: may apply to the NTIA for a grant to construct the radio access network within the State that includes the showing described in subparagraph (D); and

children.label: II
children.text: shall apply to the NTIA to lease spectrum capacity from the First Responder Network Authority.

children.heading: Disapproval
children.label: iv Disapproval
children.text: If the Commission disapproves a plan under this subparagraph, the construction, maintenance, operation, and improvements of the network within the State shall proceed in accordance with the plan proposed by the First Responder Network Authority.

children.label: I
children.text: the technical capabilities to operate, and the funding to support, the State radio access network;

children.label: II
children.text: has the ability to maintain ongoing interoperability with the nationwide public safety broadband network; and

children.label: III
children.text: the ability to complete the project within specified comparable timelines specific to the State;

children.label: ii
children.text: the cost-effectiveness of the State plan submitted under subparagraph (C)(i); and

children.label: iii
children.text: comparable security, coverage, and quality of service to that of the nationwide public safety broadband network.

children.heading: User fees
children.label: f User fees
children.text: If a State chooses to build its own radio access network, the State shall pay any user fees associated with State use of elements of the core network.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A State that chooses to build its own radio access network shall not provide commercial service to consumers or offer wholesale leasing capacity of the network within the State except directly through public-private partnerships for construction, maintenance, operation, and improvement of the network within the State.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 2 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to prohibit the State and a secondary user from entering into a covered leasing agreement. Any revenue gained by the State from such a leasing agreement shall be used only for constructing, maintaining, operating, or improving the radio access network of the State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The United States District Court for the District of Columbia shall have exclusive jurisdiction to review a decision of the Commission made under subsection (e)(3)(C)(iv).

children.label: A
children.text: the decision was procured by corruption, fraud, or undue means;

children.label: B
children.text: there was actual partiality or corruption in the Commission; or

children.label: C
children.text: the Commission was guilty of misconduct in refusing to hear evidence pertinent and material to the decision or of any other misbehavior by which the rights of any party have been prejudiced.
